content.claim: Elite gimnázium training affects approximately 200 schools and 22,000–25,000 children total according to a 2026 expert debate; defenders argue this limits aggregate equity impact, while critics counter it ignores 'pull effects' on ordinary schools and that the system originated as a path-dependent artifact of 1990s decentralization rather than deliberate gifted-education design.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: The 22,000–25,000 figure is cited as expert estimate, not official enrollment data
The 'pull effect' on ordinary schools is asserted by critics but not quantified in the notes
Historical framing (nostalgia-driven artifact vs. deliberate policy) is offered as contextual explanation but does not determine whether the system is justified today
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
